title: Phyllorkis lemniscata
intro: Phyllorkis lemniscata, also known as the 'Snake Orchid', is a flowering plant species belonging to the orchid family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Phyllorkis lemniscata include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Phyllorkis lemniscata is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae, which is the largest family of flowering plants. This classification indicates its relation to other orchids and its unique characteristics within the family. The Orchidaceae family is further divided into many subfamilies, and Phyllorkis lemniscata belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae. The Orchidoideae subfamily includes around 10,000 species of orchids, with Phyllorkis lemniscata being one of them. Understanding its classification helps in identifying its ecological and evolutionary relationships with other plants.
